title: SQL: FOREIGN KEY constraints also apply to NULL values
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE first (id INT NOT NULL, value VARCHAR(255), PRIMARY KEY (id));
CREATE TABLE second (id INT NOT NULL, ref INT, data FLOAT, PRIMARY KEY (id), FOREIGN KEY (ref) REFERENCES first(id));
INSERT INTO second (id, ref, data) VALUES (1, NULL, 3.14);
SELECT * FROM second WHERE ref IS NULL;
key_bug_features: FOREIGN KEY constraints incorrectly enforce NULL value checks
